# Public Story

NALA-DNA-Med is an open, local-first research-support project for people who want better tools around biomedical ideas, mechanisms, side effects, and hypothesis work.

The project is motivated by a concern many patients and researchers recognize: medicine can sometimes feel like symptom management with heavy tradeoffs. NALA-DNA-Med does not pretend to replace clinical science. Instead, it tries to create a transparent software foundation where ideas can be structured, sources can be preserved, and local AI can help review information without making unsupported medical promises.

The long-term hope is that researchers, clinicians, programmers, and patient advocates can build on this foundation. A programmer may not know enough biology alone. A domain expert may not want to build a whole app alone. This repository is intended to make collaboration easier.

## One-Sentence Description

NALA-DNA-Med is local-first biomedical research-support software for organizing sources, hypotheses, model workflows, and safety-aware project notes across macOS and Windows.

## Short Description for GitHub About

Local-first biomedical research-support app for macOS and Windows: CAVEMAN install, LLM-Fit, TenantShield, source archive, and safety-first hypothesis workflows.
